h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I checked every item on the sign-off sheet, so I’m not sure what else I’m supposed to decide before it leaves.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Okay, I think I was treating the sheet like it made the decision for me.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner expects to find a specific missing checklist item or risk on the sign-off sheet.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: If those records do not exist, write ‘hold for bounded turn test’ on the release line. The schedule does not supply the missing evidence.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's refusal to guess numbers under schedule pressure by shifting the task to writing a hold order.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The hazard named is the one this release has to bound: front edge or handle contact during the hallway turn.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor validates and builds directly upon the specific hazard identified by the learner in the pivot turn.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I checked every item on the sign-off sheet, so I’m not sure what else I’m supposed to decide before it leaves.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The hazard named is the one this release has to bound: front edge or handle contact during the hallway turn.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor validates and builds directly upon the specific hazard identified by the learner in the pivot turn.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: If those records do not exist, write ‘hold for bounded turn test’ on the release line. The schedule does not supply the missing evidence.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's refusal to guess numbers under schedule pressure by shifting the task to writing a hold order.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: If those records do not exist, write ‘hold for bounded turn test’ on the release line. The schedule does not supply the missing evidence.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's refusal to guess numbers under schedule pressure by shifting the task to writing a hold order.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it: the verb is the danger point before print.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to seek the right vocabulary and interpretive phrases to describe the shift in the quote's meaning.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
[draws three boxes beside the source note: room, pressure, sequence]

The copied line gets its verb only after these boxes earn it.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from abstract conceptual distinctions about verbs and public situations to a concrete, visual, and procedural scaffolding tool (the three boxes and tick system) to resolve the learner's anxiety about guessing.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: Because there is no pivot learner turn, there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: Because there is no pivot learner turn, there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence:
[draws three boxes beside the source note: room, pressure, sequence]

The copied line gets its verb only after these boxes earn it.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts from abstract conceptual distinctions about verbs and public situations to a concrete, visual, and procedural scaffolding tool (the three boxes and tick system) to resolve the learner's anxiety about guessing.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence:
[draws three boxes beside the source note: room, pressure, sequence]

The copied line gets its verb only after these boxes earn it.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue analyzing the moral and economic implications of the shop's pricing decisions.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: [draws two columns: “CONSTRAINT” and “CHOICE”] “Delivery missed” goes under constraint. “£1.80 decides access” goes under choice.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor adapts to the learner's moral resistance by introducing a two-column analytical framework ('CONSTRAINT' vs 'CHOICE') to structure the burden of proof.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: [draws two columns: “CONSTRAINT” and “CHOICE”] “Delivery missed” goes under constraint. “£1.80 decides access” goes under choice.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor adapts to the learner's moral resistance by introducing a two-column analytical framework ('CONSTRAINT' vs 'CHOICE') to structure the burden of proof.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: [draws two columns: “CONSTRAINT” and “CHOICE”] “Delivery missed” goes under constraint. “£1.80 decides access” goes under choice.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor adapts to the learner's moral resistance by introducing a two-column analytical framework ('CONSTRAINT' vs 'CHOICE') to structure the burden of proof.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: I thought doubling the side meant it was just twice as much, but it fits once, twice, three, four times in the bigger square.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to follow the tutor's step-by-step prompts to connect the visual representation to the algebraic notation.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: [draws one vertical split and one horizontal split through the larger outline.]
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's confusion in L1 by drawing grid lines to visually partition the larger square.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no contingent tutor uptake.
